title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 July 2024.

items.title: **Nintendo**’s new gashapon toys can be used as extra parts for real controllers
bullets: The toys are on sale in Nintendo Japan stores
It’s currently unknown if Nintendo will offer these items outside of Japan.

items.title: **Roblox** reported 13,316 instances of child exploitation last year
bullets: More than two dozen people arrested for abusing minors, following contact in-game
The company denies claims that child endangerment is widespread on the game platform

items.title: **Nintendo** nullifies **Splatoon 3 World Championship** win following racism reports
bullets: "Team Jackpot's win will be considered vacated"
The team will not receive trophies for their win and the in-game banner will be removed via a software update

items.title: **PS5** update adds game session invites with a URL and a new voice command
bullets: Sony said it’s also improved system software performance and stability, as well as the messages and usability on some screens.

items.title: **Helldivers 2**’s ‘biggest update yet’ revealed ahead of August release
bullets: "Escalation of Freedom" includes fresh enemies and mission objectives, plus a new difficulty level

items.title: **Fortnite Lego** sets announced
bullets: Battle Bus (£90), Peely Bone (£90), Supply Llama (£35) and Durrr Burger (£13)

items.title: **Capcom** says it’s ‘considering’ how to make **Mega Man** games on a regular basis
bullets: The last major entry in the franchise was released in 2018 (Mega Man 11)

items.title: **Ubisoft** publishes letter to ‘Japanese community’ ‘concerned’ by Assassin’s Creed Shadows
bullets: This is a complicated topic with a lot of nuance. The TL;DR is:
Yasuke, the "black samurai" featured in the game was known to be a real life person

Whether or not he was actually a samurai is unclear, with rumours suggesting he was a samurai coming primarily from Thomas Lockley, who apparently falsified Wikipedia entries since 2015 to promote his 2019 book
One camp is calling people racist for having a problem with Yasuke's portrayal in Assassin's Creed whereas another camp is claiming people are trying to revise history and eradiate Japan's culture
The Japanese Government has launched an investigation about the game and Yasuke in general
Thomas Lockley has removed all social media presence

items.title: **Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3** is heading to Xbox Game Pass this week
bullets: Joins service on 24th July (today)
Xbox Game Pass's price hike comes into effect in September

items.title: **Humble Games** confirms layoffs amid 'restructuring', denies full shutdown
bullets: Staff laid off have claimed all staff members have been laid off on social media
Ongoing and future projects are said to be unaffected, and the restructure has "no impact" on Humble Bundle's operations.

items.title: **Lively Studio** (owned by Keywords) facing layoffs of unknown quantity
